items.title: Ferdous, Sharmin take Bangladesh past Netherlands
items.summary: Netherlands however put up a dazzling display on their T20 World Cup debut to give Bangladesh a scare

items.title: 'The biggest achievement is hearing Australia praise us' - Mehidy
items.summary: Bangladesh won the series 2-1, the hosts securing their maiden ODI series win over Australia

items.title: Juairiya repays faith with match-winning fifty
items.summary: Bangladesh opener Juairiya Ferdous justified her selection in the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup squad with a scintillating half-century as her side secured a six-wicket victory over the Netherlands in their Group A opener in Birmingham on Sunday.

items.title: Shoriful's six-for in vain as Connolly's maiden ton leads Australia to thrilling win
items.summary: Cooper Connolly struck a sensational maiden ODI century as Australia survived a dramatic late scare to edge Bangladesh by one wicket in the third and final ODI at the Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium in Mirpur on Sunday.

items.title: Cooper Connolly's 149 helps Australia avoid series whitewash against Bangladesh
items.summary: The 22-year-old's maiden ODI century trumped Shoriful Islam's six-wicket haul as Australia sealed a thrilling one-wicket win in Dhaka

items.title: Fahima Khatun: "Of course I miss the spotlight in a personal way"
items.summary: In a chat with Cricbuzz, the Bangladesh all-rounder reflects on 100 T20Is, mentoring the next generation of leg-spinners, the sacrifices behind a long international career and why she has never regretted choosing cricket

items.title: Juairiya fifty on T20 World Cup debut headlines Bangladesh's winning start
items.summary: Juairiya Ferdous struck a match-winning fifty as Bangladesh overcame a mid-innings collapse to chase down a target of 140 against the Netherlands

items.title: Juairiya stars as Tigresses clinch victory in T20 World Cup opener
items.summary: Opener Juairiya Ferdous struck a blistering half-century as Bangladesh launched their ICC Women's T20 World Cup campaign in style with a six-wicket victory over the Netherlands at Edgbaston in Birmingham on Sunday.

items.title: Netherlands opt to bat against Bangladesh on T20 World Cup debut
items.summary: Netherlands captain Babette de Leede said it was a flat wicket with a fast outfield

items.title: Nayeem assault: three police officers suspended, investigation set up
items.summary: A three-member investigation committee will look into the events around the incident, which took place in Chattogram late on Friday night

items.title: Shanto leads Bangladesh in final ODI; Australia bring in Peake and Dwarshuis
items.summary: Mehidy Hasan Miraz sits out having suffered a concussion in the series-winning victory

items.title: Tigresses look to ‘bring A-game’ in WC opener
items.summary: Bangladesh will aim to open their ICC Women’s T20 World Cup campaign on a positive note when they face debutants Netherlands in their Group A opener at Edgbaston, Birmingham, today.

items.title: Connolly leads Australia recovery after Bangladesh strike early
items.summary: Cooper Connolly struck a composed half-century after Bangladesh's pace attack claimed three early wickets as Australia rebuilt their innings in pursuit of 275 in the third and final ODI at the Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium in Mirpur on Sunday.

items.title: Soumya: 'There should have been a much bigger celebration after series win'
items.summary: Bangladesh team reportedly didn't celebrate much after winning the second ODI

items.title: Bangladesh aim for 3-0 against Australia in ODI series
items.summary: Weather remains a big concern for Sunday's ODI in Mirpur

items.title: Nayeem Hasan allegedly assaulted by policemen in Chattogram
items.summary: The auto-rickshaw Nayeem Hasan was returning home from the airport in was stopped by law-enforcement officials, who proceeded to physically assault the cricketer

items.title: Mehidy taken to hospital after being hit on the head by bouncer
items.summary: Mehidy had a concussion, the team physio said, and will remain in hospital for 24 hours

items.title: Taskin, Mustafizur set up famous series win for Bangladesh
items.summary: Australia crashed to 0 for 3 and despite a century stand from Labuschagne and Bartlett, the home side came out comfortable winners

items.title: India-born Nikhil Chaudhary called into Australia T20I squad
items.summary: Former Punjab allrounder Chaudhary replaces Travis Head for Bangladesh T20Is after three excellent seasons with Hurricanes in the BBL

items.title: Taskin and Rana return for T20Is against Australia
items.summary: Towhid Hridoy and the uncapped fast-bowling allrounder Robiul Haque have been brought into the side for the one-off Test against Zimbabwe

items.title: Australia change NZ and Bangladesh schedules due to women's Champions Trophy
items.summary: New Zealand to tour twice in October and March due to February Champions Trophy while Bangladesh T20Is have been scrapped
